verdicts.note: He never names the CCA, gas taxes, or any energy policy — only unnamed 'policies he says drive up fuel and grocery prices.' Grocery prices point at general regulation, not climate policy. Mapping this to the climate axis is indirect inference; unlike an explicit fuel-tax-rollback plank, this merits only low confidence.

Sole evidence is one website line that 'local leaders should have decision-making flexibility' — a single vague phrase from one source, with no policy area attached (no zoning, GMA, or mandate positions). That is an indirect inference, not a clear statement of the local-control pole.
